content: Obesity assessment at population level includes: (1) Anthropometric measures—BMI (weight/height²), waist circumference (>102cm men, >88cm women indicates central adiposity), waist-to-hip ratio, waist-to-height ratio (>0.5 concerning); (2) Body composition—DXA scans (gold standard), bioelectrical impedance, skinfold measurements; (3) Metabolic health markers—fasting glucose, HbA1c, lipid panel, blood pressure, liver enzymes (NAFLD screening); (4) Surveillance systems—NHANES (US), BRFSS, Global Burden of Disease Study, WHO Global Health Observatory; (5) Risk stratification—Edmonton Obesity Staging System considers comorbidities, functional status, quality of life beyond BMI alone. Childhood obesity uses age/sex-specific percentiles (≥95th percentile). Limitations include self-reported data accuracy, changing demographics requiring updated reference standards, and need for better measures of adiposity and metabolic health.
